news.text: US stock futures fall as escalating Iran conflict dims rate-cut hopes - Reuters
Iran says coastal attack will lead to full Gulf closure and mine-laying - Reuters
China's Sinopec will not buy Iranian oil, wants to tap state reserves - Reuters
China warns of 'vicious cycle' if war escalates in Middle East - Reuters
Siemens CEO says customers holding back on investments due to Iran war - Reuters
Every missile fired over Iran is burning through US tungsten stocks - Reuters

brief:
◆ Market Overview

Risk-off dominance with SPY down 1.7% pre-market as Iran escalation fears spike VIX to 35.97. Tech leading the selloff at -2.27% while small caps getting hammered with IWM down 2.18%. Only financials showing green as rate cut expectations fade.

◆ What to Watch

  • Oil Supply Disruption Risk - Iran threatening Gulf closure and mine-laying operations could send energy prices parabolic
  • China's Oil Strategy - Sinopec backing away from Iranian oil, signaling potential state reserve tapping
  • Industrial Sentiment - Siemens CEO comments on investment delays due to war uncertainty
  • Tungsten Supply Shock - Military missile production burning through strategic metal reserves
⚠ Bear Case
Iran's Gulf infrastructure threats could trigger supply chain chaos. Every missile fired depletes critical tungsten stockpiles needed for defense manufacturing. Corporate investment freezes spreading globally.
⚡ Key Driver
Rate cut hopes evaporating as geopolitical risk premium demands higher yields. Fed pivot narrative dead until conflict resolution.

◆ Sector Rotation

Classic flight-to-quality underway with financials the only green sector as rising rate expectations boost bank margins. Utilities getting crushed at -4.06% as defensive plays lose appeal in crisis mode. Real estate down 3.17% on rate sensitivity and risk-off positioning.

◆ Levels That Matter

  • SPY 645 - Critical support from February lows, break triggers 630 test
  • VIX 40 - Panic threshold that historically marks short-term bottoms
  • XLE 95 - Energy breakout level if supply fears materialize
  • TNX 4.8% - 10-year yield resistance that kills growth premium
✦ Bull Case
Oversold conditions building with small caps down over 2%. China's reserve tapping comments suggest backstop for oil prices. War premiums often overshoot, creating quick mean reversion opportunities.

Not a prediction. A preparation. Make your plan before the bell.
